revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
== References == <references />
{{State Shinto}} [[Category:1872 disestablishments]] [[Category:Government agencies established in 1871]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Former government ministries of Japan|Divinities]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]]

The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
== References == <references />

The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
== References == <references />

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

{{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
== References == <references />

{{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qqq" to "qq"
revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qqq" to "qq"
revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qqq" to "qq"
revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
== References == <references />

{{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:1 revision imported]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
== References == <references />

{{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[Festival|rituals]] and [[Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
== References == <references />
{{State Shinto}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1872 disestablishments]] [[Category:Government agencies established in 1871]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Former government ministries of Japan|Divinities]] [[Category:pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ministry of Divinities]] [[en:Ministry of Divinities]]

The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[:en:Empire of Japan|Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[:en:Festival|rituals]] and [[:en:Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Taikyo Proclamation|Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis 2]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ministry of Divinities]][[ja:神祇省]][[ru:Министерство синто]][[uk:Міністерство синто]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q4294696]] Wikidata name: Ministry of Shinto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[:en:1871|1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[:en:1872|1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[:en:Empire of Japan|Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[:en:Festival|rituals]] and [[:en:Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Taikyo Proclamation|Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
== References == <references />
{{State Shinto}}

The ministry sought to help the [[Taikyo Proclamation|Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[:en:Empire of Japan|Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[:en:Festival|rituals]] and [[:en:Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Taikyo Proclamation|Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
== References == <references />

The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Taikyo Proclamation|Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ministry of Divinities]][[ja:神祇省]][[ru:Министерство синто]][[uk:Міністерство синто]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ministry of Divinities]][[ja:神祇省]][[ru:Министерство синто]][[uk:Міністерство синто]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ministry of Divinities]][[ja:神祇省]][[ru:Министерство синто]][[uk:Міністерство синто]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q4294696]] Wikidata name: Ministry of Shinto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese government agency}} {{Infobox organization | formation = [[:en:1871|1871]] | predecessor = [[Department of Divinities]] | successor = [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] | dissolved = [[:en:1872|1872]] }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the [[:en:Empire of Japan|Empire of Japan]] established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of [[:en:Festival|rituals]] and [[:en:Executive (government)|administration]] of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Taikyo Proclamation|Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the {{ill|Empire of Japan|en|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|lt=Empire of Japan}} established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|WD=Q132241|lt=rituals}} and {{ill|Executive (government)|en|Executive (government)|WD=Q35798|lt=administration}} of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Taikyo Proclamation|Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />

The {{Nihongo|'''Ministry of Divinities'''|神祇省|Jingisho}}<ref>{{Cite web |title=About Japan: A Teacher's Resource {{!}} Timeline of Religion and Nationalism in Meiji and Imperial Japan {{!}} Japan Society |url=https://aboutjapan.japansociety.org/content.cfm/timeline_of_religion_and_nationalism#sthash.qYs6daU9.dpbs |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=aboutjapan.japansociety.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://global.oup.com/academic/product/shinto-9780190621711?cc=us&lang=en& |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016-12-01 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |location=Oxford, New York}}</ref><ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: K |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html#kyobusho |access-date=2023-03-15 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> was an government organization of the {{ill|Empire of Japan|en|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|lt=Empire of Japan}} established on September 22, 1871 which lasted until April 21, 1872, replacing the [[Department of Divinities]], which had been in charge of {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|WD=Q132241|lt=rituals}} and {{ill|Executive (government)|en|Executive (government)|WD=Q35798|lt=administration}} of the Shinto gods since the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]].
The move from a Department to a Ministry was intended as a downgrade in its importance.
The ministry sought to help the [[Taikyo Proclamation|Proclamation of the Great Doctrine]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
It was quickly changed into the [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
